kurtosis_sqi.name: kurtosis_sqi
kurtosis_sqi.desc: Pearson kurtosis (non-Fisher).  Clean ECG has high kurtosis due to the narrow QRS spike: typical values 5-20 depending on segment length and HR.  Below ~2 indicates flat / saturated signal; above 30 typically indicates a single spike artifact dominating the segment.

skewness_sqi.name: skewness_sqi
skewness_sqi.desc: Sample skewness with bias correction.  Clean ECG is mildly skewed (positive in lead II, can be either sign in other leads).  |skew| > 3 typically indicates an artifact dominating one polarity.

entropy_sqi.name: entropy_sqi
entropy_sqi.desc: Shannon entropy of a 16-bin amplitude histogram, in nats.  Clean ECG is more peaked than PPG (most samples sit near baseline between QRS spikes), so its entropy is lower: typically 1.2-2.2.  Saturated or pure-noise segments fall outside this band.
entropy_sqi.ref: Empirical; calibrate via auto-tune mode for production.

snr_sqi.name: snr_sqi
snr_sqi.desc: Per-segment SNR in dB (20*log10(rms / mad)).  Clean ECG should sit higher than PPG because the QRS spike dominates the RMS while the inter-beat baseline keeps the MAD low.  Below 5 dB indicates noise-dominated; above 40 dB indicates over-filtered.

zero_crossing_sqi.name: zero_crossing_sqi
zero_crossing_sqi.desc: Zero-crossings per sample (after mean-removal).  Clean ECG should cross the mean a small number of times per cardiac cycle.  High values indicate high-frequency noise; very low values indicate a flat-line segment.
zero_crossing_sqi.ref: Derived from cardiac physiology; calibrate per recording in production.

peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi.name: peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi
peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi.desc: Raw segment peak-to-peak.  Lower bound rejects nearly-flat segments.  Upper bound is permissive — absolute ECG amplitude varies by lead and acquisition.
peak_to_peak_amplitude_sqi.ref: Heuristic; use auto-tune mode for meaningful per-recording bounds.
